steps.title: Map the logical relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1 sets the scene at the Marie Hitchcock Puppet Theater. The blank introduces "they will experience puppetry in its highest form" — describing what audiences experience at that location.

steps.title: Pick the matching transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is location-continuation. Sentence 1 places audiences at the theater; Sentence 2 describes what happens at that same place. The blank needs a transition that points back to the location just named.
  • "To that end,": signals that Sentence 2 is a purposeful action taken to achieve the goal in Sentence 1 — but Sentence 2 is not a purposeful step; it simply describes the experience at the same location. ✗
  • "However,": signals contrast — Sentence 2 is not contrary to Sentence 1; watching a puppet show and experiencing puppetry's highest form are consistent. ✗
  • "Conversely,": signals an opposite relationship — Sentence 2 continues and extends Sentence 1, not reverses it. ✗
  • "There,": signals location reference, pointing back to the theater just named — exactly what connects sitting down to enjoy a show with experiencing its highest form. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
